ops.title: Strip the Claude Code baseline from judge calls (tools=""/max_turns=1 through extract)
ops.description: WF-A item-2 caveat 2 (ledger 556d952f2): every production judge call carries a ~35–40K-token Claude Code baseline (tools + system) because the spawnllm extract path does not set tools=""/max_turns=1 (run_claude does). Per the measured cost table, per-row context is ~21–36K tokens, so stripping the baseline likely dwarfs the shipped 32% static-block cache win. Route: pass tools=""/max_turns=1 through cc_steer.claude.cached_judge's RunSpec/ClaudeConfig (or extend spawnllm extract). Verify with the WFA capture shim (~/.cc-steer/experiments/WFA/scripts/shim/claude): before/after tok/row + usd/row on a fixed slice, plus the same old-vs-new equivalence gate item 2 used (match no worse than the old-vs-old self-agreement floor).

ops.text: measured tok/row and usd/row drop on a fixed slice with envelopes attached to the WF-A ledger
equivalence gate passes (old-vs-new agreement >= old-vs-old floor, accept parity)
no PROMPT_VERSION bump unless prompt text actually changes
